meaning: Wrench or twist the ligaments of an ankle, wrist, or other joint violently.
A stretching or tearing of a muscle or a tissue connecting muscle to bone.
The cracking or breaking of a hard object or material, typically a bone.
Injury or disability caused when the normal position of a joint or other part of the body is disturbed.
An injury appearing as an area of discolored skin on the body, caused by a blow or impact.
Make an opening, incision, or wound in something with a sharp-edged tool or object.
An injury to living tissue caused by a cut, blow, or other impact.
A deep cut or tear in skin or flesh.
A mark or wound made by scratching.
An injury or mark caused by scraping.
A superficial injury caused by rubbing against a rough surface.
An injury caused by exposure to heat or flame.
Injure with very hot liquid or steam.
Temporary unconsciousness caused by a blow to the head.
Injury caused by a severe jerk to the head, typically in a motor-vehicle accident.
Inflammation of the skin caused by overexposure to ultraviolet radiation from the sun.
Injury to body tissues caused by exposure to extreme cold.
A small, thin, sharp piece of wood, glass, or similar material broken off from a larger piece.
An abnormal enlargement of a part of the body, typically as a result of an accumulation of fluid.
The escape of blood from a ruptured blood vessel, especially when external.
An escape of blood from a ruptured blood vessel, especially when internal.
A mark left on the skin or within body tissue where a wound, burn, or sore has not healed completely.
A small bubble on the skin filled with serum and caused by friction, burning, or other damage.
A thickened and hardened part of the skin or soft tissue, especially in an area that has been subjected to friction.
A painful, involuntary contraction of a muscle or muscles.
A sudden involuntary muscular contraction or convulsive movement.
